title: Ebenus andamanica
taxonomy: Ebenus andamanica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. This classification places it within the broader legume family, which is a large and diverse group of plants. Ebenus andamanica is further classified into the genus Ebenus, a small genus of flowering plants. The plant is native to the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, an archipelago in the Bay of Bengal. Within the Fabaceae family, Ebenus andamanica is part of the subfamily Faboideae, a large and widespread subfamily of legumes.
uses: Ebenus andamanica, also known as the Andaman Ebene, is a plant species native to the Andaman Islands. The plant has been traditionally used in various ways, including its leaves and roots being used for medicinal purposes. The leaves of Ebenus andamanica are believed to possess antibacterial properties, which can help in treating wounds and infections. The plant also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can be beneficial in reducing pain and swelling. Additionally, the plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever and digestive issues.

intro: Ebenus andamanica, also known as Andaman Ebony, is a deciduous tree native to the Andaman and Nicobar Islands. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Ebenus andamanica include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare various herbal remedies, including extracts, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
